regimeSub: Sticky 4.2% CPI meets a Warsh Fed dropping its cutting bias, while a US-Iran de-escalation caps oil and fuels a risk-on melt-up.
themes: US-Iran ceasefire deflates the oil risk premium -> Brent back to low-70s, energy-led CPI spike peaks
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias with CPI at 3yr high -> September HIKE risk repriced, front-end stays heavy
AI capex supercycle drives Dow above 52k and SOX -> equities ignore restrictive policy on earnings momentum
Fed independence under siege (Cook firing blocked by SCOTUS) -> term premium and gold bid as institutional risk lingers

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = negative real front end; 10y real ~flat. Fed reluctant to ease into hot prints.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp, modestly positive. Bear-steepening bias if Warsh signals hike; long-end carries fiscal/term premium.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight, no stress. VIX 17.6 and equity highs confirm risk-on; credit not pricing recession or policy error.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65, complacent. Iran deal removed left-tail; cheap hedges into a Fed that may surprise hawkish.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73.65, down post US-Iran deal. Supply-surge narrative vs ING 'overshot' — geopolitical premium drained.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $3992, near records despite no oil shock. Bid on Fed-independence threat, negative real rates, debasement hedge.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1548 weak won despite KOSPI 8528 boom; carry into JPY funding (JPYKRW 9.55) and tech inflows in tension.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/energy-trade leadership; Nikkei 70k, KOSPI 8528 confirm chip melt-up. Crowded but earnings-backed.

headline: Stocks are at record highs on a US-Iran ceasefire and an AI capex boom, but a Warsh Fed facing 4.2% inflation has killed its cutting bias and flagged a possible September hike — reflation, not landing, is the trade.
